title: Number of countries that submit data on antibiotic use in livestock
subtitle: The number of countries that submit data on the extent and quantity of antibiotic use in livestock to the World Organisation for Animal Health (WOAH). This helps to monitor the scale of antimicrobial use and risks of antimicrobial resistance.
caption:
Number of countries that submit data on antibiotic use in livestock

The number of countries that submit data on the extent and quantity of antibiotic use in livestock to the World Organisation for Animal Health (WOAH). This helps to monitor the scale of antimicrobial use and risks of antimicrobial resistance.

This line chart tracks the number of countries submitting data on antibiotic use in livestock to the World Organisation for Animal Health (WOAH) from 2014 to 2021. The vertical axis ranges from 0 to 120 countries, with horizontal grid lines marking intervals of 20. The data line begins in 2014 at a value below 60, approximately 55. A sharp increase occurs in 2015, where the count rises to just above 80. The upward trend continues through 2016 and 2017, reaching nearly 100 countries by the latter year. The line climbs further in 2018 to a point between 100 and 110, before reaching its peak in 2019 at a value slightly higher than the previous year, estimated around 113. Following this peak, the number of reporting countries declines in 2020 to a level near 105. The downward trend continues into 2021, with the final data point resting just above the 100 mark. The chart illustrates a period of growth in data submission over the first six years, followed by a decrease in the final two years of the observed period.
